# Test Fixtures

Real (or schema-accurate) match JSON used as the offline test dataset for feature extraction.

## Match Report fixtures (spec 003)

Generated by [`generate.mjs`](./generate.mjs) — deterministic, schema-accurate `match-v5` detail + timeline payloads (the same field shapes Riot returns and the app stores as `raw_json`). Player puuid is `PUUID_PLAYER` in every file. Load them with [`load.ts`](./load.ts) (`loadMatch('WIN_001')`, `loadTimeline('WIN_001')`).

| Name | Shape | Exercises |
|------|-------|-----------|
| `WIN_001` | 32-min win, player Mid (Ahri vs Zed), team ahead | core stats, matchup w/ lane opponent, gold timeline, full objective set (dragon×2, herald, baron, inhibitor), a 4-man enemy wipe before Baron, one grouped + one solo player death |
| `LOSS_002` | 30-min loss, player Mid, team behind | loss path, negative gold curve, enemy baron/inhibitor, two solo deaths |
| `SHORT_003` | 18-min win (<24 min) | "not reached" breakpoints (gold@24 → null); **no `challenges`** → kill-participation fallback `(k+a)/teamKills` |
| `JUNGLE_004` | 30-min win, player Jungle | no fixed lane opponent (`matchup.laneOpponent === null`) |

To refresh from **real** games instead: run the app once (it syncs + stores matches), then copy the `matches.raw_json` and `timelines.raw_json` rows from the dev DB over these files (research D7). The extractors and tests are agnostic to whether the JSON came from Riot or the generator — real captures additionally enable the op.gg number sanity check (SC-004).

## Other fixtures

- `opgg-lane-meta.sample.json` — OP.GG benchmark sample (spec 001).
